docs.text: A lightweight python library the Skaha Container Science Platform at CANFAR. Installation pip install skaha Example from skaha.session import Session session = Session () session . create ( name = "test" , image = "images.canfar.net/skaha/terminal:0.1" , cores = 2 , ram = 8 , kind = "headless" , cmd = "env" , env = { "TEST" : "test" }, ) Get Started Go to GitHub
docs.title: Skaha

docs.text: skaha.client.SkahaClient ( Session ) ¶ SkahaClient is the base class for all other API clients. Parameters: Name Type Description Default Session requests.Session Requests Session Object. required Exceptions: Type Description InvalidServerURL If the server URL is invalid. InvalidCertificateError If the client is given an invalid certificate. Examples: >>> from skaha.client import SkahaClient class MyClient ( SkahaClient ): pass __attrs_post_init__ ( self ) special ¶ Post Intialization Attributes. Source code in skaha/client.py def __attrs_post_init__ ( self ): """Post Intialization Attributes.""" self . headers . update ({ "Content-Type" : "application/json" }) self . headers . update ({ "Accept" : "*/*" }) self . headers . update ({ "User-Agent" : "skaha-client" }) self . headers . update ({ "Date" : asctime ( gmtime ())}) self . headers . update ({ "X-Skaha-Version" : __version__ }) self . headers . update ({ "X-Skaha-Client-Python-Version" : python_version ()}) self . headers . update ({ "X-Skaha-Client-Arch" : machine ()}) self . headers . update ({ "X-Skaha-Client-OS" : system ()}) self . headers . update ({ "X-Skaha-Client-OS-Version" : release ()}) self . headers . update ({ "X-Skaha-Client-Platform" : platform ()}) __init__ ( self , server = 'https://ws-uv.canfar.net/skaha' , certificate = '/home/runner/.ssl/cadcproxy.pem' , timeout = 15 ) special ¶ Method generated by attrs for class SkahaClient. Source code in skaha/client.py def __init__ ( self , server = attr_dict [ 'server' ] . default , certificate = attr_dict [ 'certificate' ] . default , timeout = attr_dict [ 'timeout' ] . default ): self . __attrs_pre_init__ () self . server = server self . certificate = certificate self . timeout = timeout if _config . _run_validators is True : __attr_validator_server ( self , __attr_server , self . server ) __attr_validator_certificate ( self , __attr_certificate , self . certificate ) self . __attrs_post_init__ () _check_certificate ( self , attribute , value ) private ¶ Check the certificate. Source code in skaha/client.py @certificate . validator def _check_certificate ( self , attribute , value ): """Check the certificate.""" if not Path ( value ) . is_absolute (): raise InvalidCertificateError ( "certificate must be an absolute path." ) if not Path ( value ) . is_file (): raise InvalidCertificateError ( f " { value } does not exist." ) self . headers . update ({ "X-Skaha-Authentication-Type" : "certificate" }) self . cert = value self . verify = True _check_server ( self , attribute , value ) private ¶ Check if server is a valid url. Source code in skaha/client.py @server . validator def _check_server ( self , attribute , value ): """Check if server is a valid url.""" if not url ( value ): raise InvalidServerURL ( "Server must be a valid URL." ) self . headers . update ({ "X-Skaha-Server" : value })

docs.text: skaha.context.Context ( SkahaClient ) ¶ Get available resources from the skaha server. resources ( self ) ¶ Get available resources from the skaha server. Returns: Type Description dict A dictionary of available resources. Examples: >>> from skaha.context import Context context = Context () context . resources () >>> { 'defaultCores' : 2 , 'defaultCoresHeadless' : 1 , ... } Source code in skaha/context.py def resources ( self ) -> dict : """Get available resources from the skaha server. Returns: A dictionary of available resources. Examples: >>> from skaha.context import Context context = Context() context.resources() >>> {'defaultCores': 2, 'defaultCoresHeadless': 1, ...} """ return self . get ( url = self . server ) . json ()

docs.text: skaha.images.Images ( SkahaClient ) ¶ Skaha Image Management. Parameters: Name Type Description Default SkahaClient object Skaha HTTP Client required fetch ( self , kind = None , prune = True ) ¶ Get images from Skaha Server. Parameters: Name Type Description Default kind Optional[str] Type of image. Defaults to None. None prune bool Provide only image name. Defaults to True. True Returns: Type Description list A list of images on the skaha server. Examples: >>> from skaha.images import Images images = Images () images . fetch ( kind = "headless" ) >>> [ 'images.canfar.net/chimefrb/baseband-polarization:latest' , ... 'images.canfar.net/skaha/terminal:0.1' ] Source code in skaha/images.py def fetch ( self , kind : Optional [ str ] = None , prune : bool = True ) -> list : """Get images from Skaha Server. Args: kind (Optional[str], optional): Type of image. Defaults to None. prune (bool, optional): Provide only image name. Defaults to True. Returns: list: A list of images on the skaha server. Examples: >>> from skaha.images import Images images = Images() images.fetch(kind="headless") >>> ['images.canfar.net/chimefrb/baseband-polarization:latest', ... 'images.canfar.net/skaha/terminal:0.1'] """ data : dict = {} if kind : data [ "type" ] = kind response = self . get ( url = self . server , params = data ) . json () if prune : for index , image in enumerate ( response ): response [ index ] = image [ "id" ] return response
docs.title: Images

Args: kind (Optional[str], optional): Type of image. Defaults to None. prune (bool, optional): Provide only image name. Defaults to True. Returns: list: A list of images on the skaha server. Examples: >>> from skaha.images import Images images = Images() images.fetch(kind="headless") >>> ['images.canfar.net/chimefrb/baseband-polarization:latest', ... 'images.canfar.net/skaha/terminal:0.1'] """ data : dict = {} if kind : data [ "type" ] = kind response = self . get ( url = self . server , params = data ) . json () if prune : for index , image in enumerate ( response ): response [ index ] = image [ "id" ] return response

docs.text: skaha.session.Session ( SkahaClient ) ¶ Skaha Session Client. fetch ( self , kind = None , status = None , view = None ) ¶ List open sessions for the user. Parameters: Name Type Description Default kind str Session kind. Defaults to None. None status str Session status. Defaults to None. None view str Session view level. Defaults to None. None Exceptions: Type Description ParameterError When kind , status or view are malformed. Returns: Type Description list Sessions information. Examples: >>> from skaha.session import Session session = Session () session . fetch ( kind = "desktop" ) Source code in skaha/session.py @beartype def fetch ( self , kind : KIND = None , status : STATUS = None , view : VIEW = None , ) -> list : """List open sessions for the user. Args: kind (str, optional): Session kind. Defaults to None. status (str, optional): Session status. Defaults to None. view (str, optional): Session view level. Defaults to None. Raises: ParameterError: When `kind`, `status` or `view` are malformed. Returns: list: Sessions information. Examples: >>> from skaha.session import Session session = Session() session.fetch(kind="desktop") """ params : dict = {} if kind : params [ "type" ] = kind if status : params [ "status" ] = status if view : params [ "view" ] = view log . debug ( params ) response = self . get ( url = self . server , params = params ) response . raise_for_status () return response . json () info ( self , id ) ¶ Get information about a session. Parameters: Name Type Description Default ids str Session ID. required Returns: Type Description str Session information. Examples: >>> session . info ( id = "hjko98yghj" ) Source code in skaha/session.py def info ( self , id : ID ) -> str : """Get information about a session. Args: ids (str): Session ID. Returns: str: Session information. Examples: >>> session.info(id="hjko98yghj") """ response = self . get ( url = self . server + "/" + id , params = { "view" : "event" }) response . raise_for_status () return response . text logs ( self , id ) ¶ Get logs from a session. Parameters: Name Type Description Default id str Session ID. required Returns: Type Description str Logs in text/plain format. Examples: >>> session . logs ( id = "hjko98yghj" ) Source code in skaha/session.py def logs ( self , id : ID ) -> List [ str ]: """Get logs from a session. Args: id (str): Session ID. Returns: str: Logs in text/plain format. Examples: >>> session.logs(id="hjko98yghj") """ response = self . get ( url = self . server + "/" + id , params = { "view" : "logs" }) response . raise_for_status () return response . text . split ( " \n " ) create ( self , name , image , cores = 1 , ram = 4 , kind = None , cmd = None , args = None , env = None ) ¶ Launch a skaha session. Parameters: Name Type Description Default name str The name for the session. required image str The Image ID of the session. e.g. images.canfar.net/skaha/notebook-scipy:0.2 required cores int Number of cores. Defaults to 1. 1 ram int Amount of RAM. Defaults to 4. 4 kind str Session kind for images not from harbor registries. Defaults to None. None cmd str Override the image entrypoint. Defaults to None. None args str Override the image CMD params. Defaults to None. None env dict Environment variables. Defaults to None. None Returns: Type Description str Session ID. Examples: >>> session . create ( name = "test" , image = "images.canfar.net/skaha/terminal:0.1" , cores = 2 , ram = 8 , kind = "headless" , cmd = "env" , env = { "TEST" : "test" } ) >>> "hjko98yghj" Source code in skaha/session.py def create ( self , name : NAME , image : IMAGE , cores : CORE = 1 , ram : RAM = 4 , kind : KIND = None , cmd : CMD = None , args : ARG = None , env : ENV = None , ) -> str : """Launch a skaha session. Args: name (str): The name for the session. image (str): The Image ID of the session. e.g. images.canfar.net/skaha/notebook-scipy:0.2 cores (int, optional): Number of cores. Defaults to 1. ram (int, optional): Amount of RAM. Defaults to 4. kind (str, optional): Session kind for images not from harbor registries. Defaults to None. cmd (str, optional): Override the image entrypoint. Defaults to None. args (str, optional): Override the image CMD params. Defaults to None. env (dict, optional): Environment variables. Defaults to None. Returns: str: Session ID. Examples: >>> session.create( name="test", image="images.canfar.net/skaha/terminal:0.1", cores=2, ram=8, kind="headless", cmd="env", env={"TEST": "test"} ) >>> "hjko98yghj" """ data : dict = { "name" : name , "image" : image , "cores" : cores , "ram" : ram } params : dict = {} if kind : params [ "type" ] = kind # Command, arguments and evironment variables are only supported for # headless sessions. if kind == "headless" : if cmd : params [ "cmd" ] = cmd if args : params [ "args" ] = args if env : params [ "env" ] = env log . info ( params ) response = self . post ( url = self . server , data = data , params = params ) response . raise_for_status () return response . text . rstrip ( " \r\n " ) destroy ( self , id ) ¶ Destroy a skaha session. Parameters: Name Type Description Default id str (str): Session ID. required Returns: Type Description bool True if the session was destroyed. Examples: >>> session . destroy ( id = "hjko98yghj" ) Source code in skaha/session.py def destroy ( self , id : str ) -> bool : """Destroy a skaha session. Args: id: (str): Session ID. Returns: bool: True if the session was destroyed. Examples: >>> session.destroy(id="hjko98yghj") """ response = self . delete ( url = self . server + "/" + id ) response . raise_for_status () if response . status_code == 200 : return True return False

Returns: str: Logs in text/plain format. Examples: >>> session.logs(id="hjko98yghj") """ response = self . get ( url = self . server + "/" + id , params = { "view" : "logs" }) response . raise_for_status () return response . text . split ( " \n " )

docs.text: Launch a skaha session. Parameters: Name Type Description Default name str The name for the session. required image str The Image ID of the session. e.g. images.canfar.net/skaha/notebook-scipy:0.2 required cores int Number of cores. Defaults to 1. 1 ram int Amount of RAM. Defaults to 4. 4 kind str Session kind for images not from harbor registries. Defaults to None. None cmd str Override the image entrypoint. Defaults to None. None args str Override the image CMD params. Defaults to None. None env dict Environment variables. Defaults to None. None Returns: Type Description str Session ID. Examples: >>> session . create ( name = "test" , image = "images.canfar.net/skaha/terminal:0.1" , cores = 2 , ram = 8 , kind = "headless" , cmd = "env" , env = { "TEST" : "test" } ) >>> "hjko98yghj" Source code in skaha/session.py def create ( self , name : NAME , image : IMAGE , cores : CORE = 1 , ram : RAM = 4 , kind : KIND = None , cmd : CMD = None , args : ARG = None , env : ENV = None , ) -> str : """Launch a skaha session. Args: name (str): The name for the session. image (str): The Image ID of the session. e.g. images.canfar.net/skaha/notebook-scipy:0.2 cores (int, optional): Number of cores. Defaults to 1. ram (int, optional): Amount of RAM. Defaults to 4. kind (str, optional): Session kind for images not from harbor registries. Defaults to None. cmd (str, optional): Override the image entrypoint. Defaults to None. args (str, optional): Override the image CMD params. Defaults to None. env (dict, optional): Environment variables. Defaults to None. Returns: str: Session ID. Examples: >>> session.create( name="test", image="images.canfar.net/skaha/terminal:0.1", cores=2, ram=8, kind="headless", cmd="env", env={"TEST": "test"} ) >>> "hjko98yghj" """ data : dict = { "name" : name , "image" : image , "cores" : cores , "ram" : ram } params : dict = {} if kind : params [ "type" ] = kind # Command, arguments and evironment variables are only supported for # headless sessions. if kind == "headless" : if cmd : params [ "cmd" ] = cmd if args : params [ "args" ] = args if env : params [ "env" ] = env log . info ( params ) response = self . post ( url = self . server , data = data , params = params ) response . raise_for_status () return response . text . rstrip ( " \r\n " )
docs.title: create()

docs.text: Destroy a skaha session. Parameters: Name Type Description Default id str (str): Session ID. required Returns: Type Description bool True if the session was destroyed. Examples: >>> session . destroy ( id = "hjko98yghj" ) Source code in skaha/session.py def destroy ( self , id : str ) -> bool : """Destroy a skaha session. Args: id: (str): Session ID. Returns: bool: True if the session was destroyed. Examples: >>> session.destroy(id="hjko98yghj") """ response = self . delete ( url = self . server + "/" + id ) response . raise_for_status () if response . status_code == 200 : return True return False
